channel.title: 2000 on Modest Walrus
channel.description: Recent content in 2000 on Modest Walrus

item.title: Reaction Quality Control by Jurassic 5
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Album

Jurassic 5 have a reputation as one of those groups people point to when they say they “don’t really like hip-hop, but…” Quality Control is the album that earns that reputation, so it was worth revisiting properly.

The Video

About This Album

Quality Control is the second studio album by American alternative hip hop group Jurassic 5, released on June 6, 2000, by Interscope Records. At Metacritic, which assigns a normalized rating out of 100 to reviews from professional publications, the album received an average score of 77 based on 17 reviews, indicating “generally favorable reviews.” In 2015, it was included in Paste’s list of “10 Hip-Hop Albums for People Who Don’t Like Hip-Hop.”

item.title: Reaction How to Disappear Completely by Radiohead
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

It came on as a radio follow-up and it’s just so haunting. The strings pulled me in immediately and I couldn’t skip past it.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“How to Disappear Completely” appears on Radiohead’s 2000 album Kid A, produced by the band with Nigel Godrich. Thom Yorke wrote it after a moment of dissociation on tour, taking advice from Michael Stipe to mentally repeat “I’m not here, this isn’t happening” to get through overwhelming situations. Jonny Greenwood arranged the strings, performed with the Orchestra of St John’s, building the song around a detuned guitar drone beneath the orchestration.

item.title: Reaction The Light by Common
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

I was blown away from the show. Seeing Common perform live put this song in a completely different light and it’s stuck with me ever since.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“The Light” was released in 2000 on Common’s album Like Water for Chocolate, produced by James Poyser and built around a sample of Bobby Caldwell’s “Open Your Eyes.” Part of the Soulquarians-era work that defined the album’s sound, it became one of Common’s biggest and most accessible singles; a warm, melodic love song that stood apart from the more conscious, abstract material he was known for at the time.

item.title: Gaming: Counter-Strike
item.description:
Game/Publisher/Developer

Counter-Strike, Valve / Sierra Studios, Valve (originally a Half-Life mod by Minh Le and Jess Cliffe)

The Trailer

My Reaction

Counter-Strike was very unique in 2000. Multiplayer gaming was still in an early stage and Counter-Strike changed the game completely. We played it in the dorms over T1, and it was unlike anything else; a tactical team-based shooter when most multiplayer shooters were still pure run-and-gun. I was into it.
